Flayed Paebala Gloves  
 

Lore Item No Trade
Slot: HANDS
AC: 12
DEX: +15
SV FIRE: +5 SV DISEASE: +5 SV COLD: +5 SV MAGIC: +5 SV POISON: +5
Focus: Reanimation Haste II
WT: 1.0 Size: TINY
Class: ALL except WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Flayed Paebala Gloves
Lore Item No Trade
Slot: HANDS
AC: 12
DEX: +15
SV FIRE: +5 SV DISEASE: +5 SV COLD: +5 SV MAGIC: +5 SV POISON: +5
Focus: Reanimation Haste II
WT: 1.0 Size: TINY
Class: ALL except WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Lore:Paebala Skin Gloves
Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Velious Leather 1
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):20, 20, 20
Stackable:No
Lucy Entry By:Tiana
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-12-13 07:50:38
Page Updated:Fri Sep 20th, 2013

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 53
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Dragon Necropolis
NPC Name
a Chetari Dominator
a Chetari master
Dominator Yisaki


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Dragon Necropolis


Uploaded March 18th, 2025 by iventheassassin
Send a correction
Post Comment
Focus effect...
QuoteReply
# Oct 16 2002 at 4:52 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965862038
View User Forum
86 posts
Absolutely sucks. Level 2 is worthless to any necromancer above lvl 49. DN, I think, is good for mid 50s and above, not saying that lower cannot go. But you don't see a lot of lvl 40-44 characters sitting at zone shouting to loot stuff.
▲ Page top
ranger guants
QuoteReply
# Sep 15 2002 at 10:04 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591770980837
24 posts
these should go to a ranger with am3 they die for all that dex.
▲ Page top
RE: ranger guants
QuoteReply
# Feb 19 2003 at 2:51 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592757586331
Scholar
63 posts
N/nod. As a 63 Ranger, I find these to be excellent equipment. On raids, the saves are great. On raids, the dex is perfect.
Its been quite a few levels since I could go toe to toe with any worthwhile mob. With the PoP mobs hitting for 300+ and often 800, if the tank goes down, then another 5 or 10 AC will not help me much. What's 3 melee rounds going to do? I can get more time for the gaters by simply snaring and dragging the beast to, well, to my death, I suppose. But the added dex has a real impact on bow crits and melee procs. Since a Ranger uses self buff weapon procs, this is always important.
If you're a Ranger and your guild can do DN, go with them there. You'll likely be one of the only ones to roll on a fairly common drop.
▲ Page top
RE: ranger guants
QuoteReply
# Nov 30 2002 at 1:59 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592247010486
Scholar
360 posts
You, sir, are correct.
▲ Page top
Enhancement Haste III
QuoteReply
# Sep 04 2002 at 9:09 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251924332
Scholar
303 posts
While enhancement haste might not seem too important at first given that most buffing is done during down times, one notable benefit from this focus effect is quicker casting of HP/AC buffs. Speaking as a paladin, some of my HP/AC buffs such as Valor are more mana-efficient for healing than my actual healing spells. Having a quicker cast time would be nice during the root-and-heal situations. Even without the focus effect, they are nice gloves in general.

Edited, Wed Sep 4 09:59:28 2002
▲ Page top
Who drops these?
QuoteReply
# Mar 15 2002 at 6:29 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591773916956
View User Forum
Sage
164 posts
I haven't ventured into Dragon Necropolis yet because I'm still a few levels too low, but I am adding these gloves to my wish list.

Who or what drops them? How hard is he to kill?
▲ Page top
RE: Who drops these?
QuoteReply
# May 28 2002 at 9:43 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591762323414
109 posts
Guild (Clan of One) on BB hit DN the other night, these gloves dropped off of "A Chetari Master".

I'm a bard (48 atm) and these are excellent. 4 less ac than my SS Gaunts, but...I'm not a tank =P Bards and Rogues are one step up from Rangers. Don't want to put me in front of an "ENRAGED" or "Flurry" mob, so this is actually a well needed upgrade. All that Dex, all those saves...will help me when I get to Tag along in the ToV raids. Also the dex will help my Smphonic Sabre go off more, I realize there are better weapons but the Aggro reducing proc on it will help me "Not Die" as much as would happen w/o it =)

Gallowbraid (51 SK) "Clan of One"
Jhoval (48 Bard) "Clan of One"
~Bristlebane~

LoL, another (Ranger Name)'s Corpe...When will they learn =)
▲ Page top

Quote: LoL, another (Ranger Name)'s Corpe...When will they learn =)

__DEL__1592246190773
158 posts
The ac on these suck lol... are you kidding me? Its only 12. The resists are sweet tho and so is the dexterity. But not good for tanking. I am a warrior. I wouldnt use these except on dragon raids etc where you need all the resists you can get.
▲ Page top
RE: bard
QuoteReply
# Jan 15 2001 at 4:26 AM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Like what was said before, these are bard/rogue gloves. We aren't meant to take the hits, we're meant to dish out the damage. In a bards case, this will help us with missed notes, and more proc's....indeed a nice item for bard.
▲ Page top
Necro
QuoteReply
# Dec 16 2000 at 3:13 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Why can Necros use these? Doesn't make a lot of sense...
▲ Page top
RE: Necro
QuoteReply
# Oct 16 2002 at 4:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965862038
View User Forum
86 posts
Necros can use these because the mobs that these gloves drop from are necros, etc. If they can wear them so can other necs. The Chetari are the evil race of rats that have gained power from eating the dead flesh of dragons. Paebala are the race of not so evil rats that are opposed to the Chetari. So the Chetari turn them into fashionware.
▲ Page top
RE: Necro
QuoteReply
# Dec 16 2000 at 8:59 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
These are basically an update to the flayed skin armor set from kunark (human and erudite necro only). The pieces of that set all had high ac and decent melee type stats, but very little in the way of intelligence bonuses. Helps a lot to have high ac as a necro. 200 int doesn't help much if you get beat down before you can use even half your mana.
▲ Page top
RE: Necro
QuoteReply
# Dec 16 2000 at 3:25 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
And a second question just like this
Why would Necros WANT to use these?
▲ Page top
RE: Necro
QuoteReply
# Apr 25 2002 at 4:06 PM Rating: Excellent
kthomas
50 posts
OK, lvl 50 necromancer of lanys tval. current INT=224 or a little higher, and in hands, I am using gem inlaid gaunts, ac 8 agi dex 3. not the greatest of gaunts, but guess what, I dont really need better, unless its these, i havent scoured these loot pages trying to find a decent set of gloves, but these thigns would rock for me, high dex? well, i believe it helps the channeling and wutnot when getting hit. I have personally lost all dex items and tried to cast when getting hit a couple times, then i get a dex buff and find some dex gear, and everythign seems much harder to interupt. just my 2cp.

Faumin Cometchaser
Erudite Necromancer of the 50th circle
____________________________

▲ Page top
RE: Necro
QuoteReply
# Dec 16 2000 at 3:44 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592245623402
6 posts
Know any other gloves with + 5 to all resist that casters can use? Some very high AC (for caster gloves..) not bad either.. and i am pretty sure some necros still have well over 200 int even when puting their resist gear and remooving their gloves.
I would undoubtedly remove my impskin gloves, or even apothic gloves if i had them, for these. Too bad it's nt wearable by mages.

Ektarot, mage 55, MM.

Proof?
QuoteReply
# May 22 2001 at 1:07 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Can you kindly give us a link to where you heard that Dex affects casting?

I always thought that's why casters had a channeling skill personally.

And this is taken directly from Castersrealm:

"Dexterity: Dexterity affects how quickly you learn weapon skills, and how often weapons will do their special. This really only has any relevance to Necromancers at lower levels when they are forced to melee. You should not put points into this skill at creation."
